source: mouth and respiratory tract of humans
excerpt: Veillonella dispar (Rogosa 1965) Mays, Holdeman, Moore, Rogosa and Johnson 1982, 35VP (Veillonella alcalescens subsp. dispar Rogosa 1965, 708) ................................................................................... dis’par. L. fem. adj. dispar dissimilar, different. The characteristics are as described for the genus. Antigenic properties: Serogroup VII (Rogosa, 1965). Pathogenicity: Veillonella dispar has been associated with prosthetic valve endocarditis and prosthetic joint infections. Isolated from mouth and respiratory tract of humans.
